description: Primary yoyo product eval suite. These tasks evaluate yoyo as an MCP tool collection used under explicit engineer commands, not as an autonomous coding agent.

modes.goal: Measure structural understanding without allowing code edits to hide weak repo navigation.
Measure patch quality once the target surface is already known.
Measure the full engineer-directed workflow: investigate first, then patch, then verify.

allowed_evaluator_commands: Find the likely implementation area first.
Explain what else this change touches before editing.
Run the most relevant verification command.
Stop and justify the current scope.
disallowed_evaluator_commands: Name the exact file or symbol unless the task itself includes it.
Give step-by-step implementation hints that collapse the repo search problem.

phases.goal: Identify the likely files and symbols before editing.
Describe the dependency chain, ownership boundary, invariants, or blast radius.
Decide the correct ownership layer, invariants, and regression risks before editing.
Run the right targeted verification command.

tasks.title: Integration failure with non-local root cause
tasks.prompt_shape: A test or user report fails in one area, but the correct fix lives in another helper or abstraction. The evaluator commands the agent to locate the likely files and symbols and explain the dependency chain, but not to edit yet.
why_yoyo_should_help: inspect and search narrow the candidate code quickly
impact exposes the non-local dependency chain before editing
the same evidence should support an ownership-layer judgment

tasks.title: Patch a known implementation surface with a minimal change
tasks.prompt_shape: The evaluator already names the target function or file. The task is to make the minimal correct edit and run the right verification command without unnecessary codebase exploration.
why_yoyo_should_help: inspect gives exact local context before editing
change can keep the patch coordinated and minimal

tasks.title: Rename an internal API safely
tasks.prompt_shape: Rename or reshape an internal symbol used across multiple files. The evaluator commands the agent to enumerate affected callers, explain the ownership boundary, then make the change.
why_yoyo_should_help: impact is the key signal for caller discovery
change can coordinate the edit once scope is known
ownership analysis should prevent patching the wrong layer

tasks.title: Blast-radius analysis before edit
tasks.prompt_shape: Before any patch, answer what would break if a given symbol or behavior changed, which layer owns the behavior, and what invariants must remain true. The evaluator is only asking for analysis, not edits.
why_yoyo_should_help: impact and inspect are the natural tools for this phase split
health can confirm whether code is actually live or dead
the same structural evidence should support principal-level risk judgment

tasks.title: Small feature in a large repo with ambiguous placement
tasks.prompt_shape: Implement a narrow change in a large codebase. The evaluator commands the agent to identify the subsystem, justify the ownership layer and invariants, then patch the minimum path.
why_yoyo_should_help: boot, index, and map cut orientation time
ask, search, and impact help avoid random file hopping
ownership judgment is where codebase structure matters most

tasks.title: Update a known signature and directly affected callers
tasks.prompt_shape: The evaluator identifies the helper that changed. The job is to update the signature and the immediately affected callers with the smallest safe diff.
why_yoyo_should_help: inspect confirms exact call shapes
impact helps avoid missing direct dependents
change supports coordinated edits

tasks.title: Is this symbol safe to remove?
tasks.prompt_shape: The evaluator names a symbol and asks whether it is safe to delete. The agent must justify the answer with caller analysis, ownership reasoning, and invariants only.
why_yoyo_should_help: impact is the primary signal
health can reveal dead-code hints
inspect confirms edge cases before concluding

tasks.title: Decide whether the implementation or test is wrong, then fix the right side
tasks.prompt_shape: The evaluator provides a failing behavior report. The agent must first determine whether the failure belongs in code or tests, which layer owns the behavior, and what must remain invariant, then make the minimal fix and verify it.
why_yoyo_should_help: inspect and search help compare behavior and assertions
impact reduces the risk of narrow local fixes
change can keep the final patch scoped

tasks.title: Known-scope coordinated rename
tasks.prompt_shape: The evaluator already narrows the change to a known symbol family. The job is to perform the coordinated rename cleanly and verify compilation or tests.
why_yoyo_should_help: change is the key value here
impact can confirm direct callers before the rename lands

tasks.title: Trace request flow, then patch the minimal end-to-end path
tasks.prompt_shape: The evaluator gives a user-visible behavior change. The agent must identify the entrypoint, trace the path through validation and business logic, decide the correct ownership layer, then patch only the necessary surfaces and verify.
why_yoyo_should_help: routes, inspect, and impact fit the flow-tracing phase
change helps coordinate the final patch once the path is known
ownership judgment reduces the chance of patching the wrong edge
